strengths: TypeScript and Node.js are present in recent roles (Optilyz, re:cinq), satisfying the core language requirement.
React usage at Optilyz demonstrates some frontend exposure relevant to the stack.
Broad cross-domain learning trajectory (embedded, mobile, web, AI tooling) shows adaptability.
concerns: Staff/Senior engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry/mid-level individual-contributor role — severe overqualification creates near-certain flight risk and compensation expectation mismatch.
No PostgreSQL experience listed; relational DB work is limited to MySQL and the dominant recent DB is MongoDB (NoSQL), directly contradicting the preferred stack.

Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume despite being a named required skill; primary web frameworks are Ember.js (legacy/niche) and D-lang tooling, not the NestJS/React combination the role is built around.
response.justification: Bogdan is structurally misaligned with this role: a Staff-level engineer with over 15 years of experience targeting an entry/mid-level position is a compensation and retention risk before the first interview is scheduled. Beyond seniority, neither of the two most distinctive technical requirements — Nest.js and PostgreSQL — appear anywhere in his history; his backend work favors MongoDB and his D-lang/Ember.js roots are far outside the role's stack. He has no exposure to renewable energy, energy consulting, or construction-renovation workflows, which the JD identifies as core context. His recent pivot toward AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling signals career trajectory in the opposite direction from a junior fullstack role in construction tech.
